title: blechnum pygmaeum
intro: Blechnum pygmaeum, also known as the dwarf chain fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Blechnaceae. It is native to Australia and is known for its potential medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Blechnum pygmaeum can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or infusions for medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
